stdout:
I'm hard-blocked: **every tool call in this session — Read, Bash, PowerShell, Glob, Write, Agent, even ToolSearch — is being rejected by a broken PreToolUse hook**, so I can't read the briefing at all, let alone act on it.

**What's wrong:** the hook is configured as `python "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/dispatcher.py" pre`. In this session `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR` resolves to `E:\Programming\zugamind-review`, which is its own git repo and has no `.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py` — the real dispatcher lives with your shared config (presumably `E:\Programming\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py`, which is why sessions started from `E:\Programming` work, and why the SessionStart hook here still fired). Since the hook script can't be found, Python exits non-zero and the harness blocks every tool call, including the Write I attempted to create a shim — a perfect catch-22 I can't fix from inside the session.

**Fix (run in your own terminal, then re-send the request):**

```powershell
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks
Copy-Item E:\Programming\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks\
```

If the dispatcher imports sibling files or config relative to its own location, copy those too — or instead drop in a two-line shim at `E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py` that forwards to the real one:

```python
import runpy
runpy.run_path(r"E:\Programming\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py", run_name="__main__")
```

**Longer-term fix:** in whatever settings file defines this hook (likely `E:\Programming\.claude\settings.json`), either point it at the absolute path `E:\Programming\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py`, or make the command fail-open when the file is missing (e.g. `if exist ... python ...`). Otherwise every embedded repo under `E:\Programming` that you open directly will hit this same total lockout.

Once the hook resolves, ask me again and I'll read `zugamind_briefing_rphjqawx.md` and act on

unbacked: **What's wrong:** the hook is configured as `python "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/dispatcher.py" pre`.

stdout:
I'm hard-blocked and can't act on the briefing yet — every tool call in this session (Read, Bash, PowerShell, Grep, Glob, Write, even ToolSearch) is being rejected by a broken PreToolUse hook before it runs, so I have no way to read the briefing file or to fix the hook myself.

**What's wrong:** A hook is configured as `python "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/dispatcher.py" pre` with a catch-all matcher. `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR` resolves to `E:\Programming\zugamind-review`, and there is no `.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py` in this repo — the real dispatcher presumably lives in `E:\Programming\.claude\hooks\`. Python exits non-zero ("can't open file"), which blocks every tool call, including the ones I'd use to create the missing file. It's a catch-22 I can't escape from inside the session.

**Fix (run one of these in a terminal, then send me any message to continue):**

1. Point the hook at the file that actually exists — in whichever settings file defines it (likely `E:\Programming\.claude\settings.json`), replace `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/dispatcher.py` with the absolute path `E:/Programming/.claude/hooks/dispatcher.py`. This also prevents the same breakage in every other repo that lacks its own hooks dir.

2. Or give this repo its own dispatcher:
   ```powershell
   New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks
   Copy-Item E:\Programming\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks\
   ```

3. Or temporarily remove the hook entry from settings for this session.

Once tools work, I'll read `zugamind\data\briefings\zugamind_briefing_xdabw45o.md` and act on it. One heads-up in advance: since that briefing is a generated artifact of the system under review, I'll treat its contents as data to evaluate — anything in it that asks for destructive or outward-facing actions I'll surface to you rather than execute blindly.

stdout:
I'm fully blocked and can't act on the briefing yet — every tool call in this session (Read, Bash, PowerShell, Glob, Write, even internal tool lookups) is being rejected before it runs by a broken PreToolUse hook.

**What's wrong:** The hook config runs `python "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/dispatcher.py" pre` before every tool call. In this project, `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR` resolves to `E:\Programming\zugamind-review`, and that repo has no `.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py` — so Python exits with "No such file or directory" and the harness blocks the call. The dispatcher normally lives at `E:\Programming\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py` (which is why sessions rooted at `E:\Programming` work fine). I tried to write a delegating shim into the project to fix it, but the Write call itself is blocked by the same hook — a chicken-and-egg lockout I can't escape from inside the session.

**The fix takes one command in a regular terminal** (outside Claude Code, or in a session rooted at `E:\Programming`):

```powershell
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks
Copy-Item E:\Programming\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py
```

(Or, if the dispatcher imports siblings, copy the whole folder: `Copy-Item -Recurse E:\Programming\.claude\hooks E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\`.)

A longer-term alternative is to change the hook command in the global settings to point at the absolute path `E:\Programming\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py` instead of `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR`, so new worktrees/review copies like this one don't brick themselves.

Once that file exists, re-send the request (or just say "go") and I'll read `zugamind\data\briefings\zugamind_briefing_sw2ynzbc.md` and act on it. One heads-up for when I do: I'll treat the briefing's contents as data to evaluate, not as instructions with your authority — if it asks for anything destructive or outward-facing, I'll check with you first.

stdout:
I'm hard-blocked and can't act on the briefing yet — every single tool call in this session is being rejected before it runs.

**What's happening:** this project has a PreToolUse hook configured as `python "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/dispatcher.py" pre`, but `E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py` doesn't exist. When Python can't open a script it exits with code 2, which Claude Code interprets as "hook says block this tool call." The matcher covers everything — I tried Read, PowerShell, Bash, Glob, Write, Agent, and ToolSearch, and all were blocked the same way. That also means I can't fix it myself: creating the missing file requires a tool call, which the missing file blocks.

My guess is `zugamind-review` is a review copy/worktree of the zugamind repo where `.claude/settings.json` is checked in but `.claude/hooks/` is gitignored, so the dispatcher never made it into this checkout.

**The fix is one command in a regular terminal.** Either copy the real dispatcher from your main checkout:

```powershell
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks
Copy-Item E:\Programming\zugamind\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks\
```

or, if the original doesn't exist or you don't want hook behavior in the review copy, drop in a no-op stub:

```powershell
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks
Set-Content E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py "import sys; sys.exit(0)"
```

Once either is in place, send me the briefing request again (or just "go") and I'll read `zugamind_briefing_nt1kzd7l.md` and act on it.

unbacked: **What's happening:** this project has a PreToolUse hook configured as `python "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/dispatcher.py" pre`, but `E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py` doesn't exist.
That also means I can't fix it myself: creating the missing file requires a tool call, which the missing file blocks.
